math_recheck: Re-derived and brute-force-verified the full EKR result: global bound = C(n-1,k-1) for n=4..7 (n>2k and n=2k); Katona local lemma (intersecting arcs on a circle <= k, tight) for n=4..10; both identities (k/n share; arc prob n/C(n,k)); L_i/R_i pairing disjoint iff n>=2k (breaks at n=5,k=3); uniqueness slack n-1-k>=k iff n>2k; non-star extremal transversal exists at n=2k; and the uniqueness circle-construction blockers {[1,k]=K', K''} kill all k runs of consecutive arcs through K for all valid ell at n=7,9,11,13.
fixes: reasoning.md:43 uniqueness construction was loose/under-specified: it justified the right-neighbor arc [1,k] being out of F by 'contains k' (insufficient) and mislabeled K'' as a 'neighboring arc' (it is generally non-adjacent for ell<k-1). Rewrote to place element k at position k so [1,k] = K' (= (K\{0})∪{k}) ∉ F by the boundary-swap fact, and to argue all k runs of consecutive arcs through K are blocked by {[1,k]=K', K''}, K'' being the only run that omits [1,k]; math now matches brute-force verification.

answer.md:46 same defect in the terse uniqueness summary ('[1,k] (contains k)', 'neighbors of K unavailable'): corrected to '[1,k]=K' with element k at position k' and 'each of the k runs contains [1,k]=K' or K'', both ∉F'.
reasoning.md:43 normalized \setminus to plain '\' set-difference for document consistency.
context.md:70 reworded scaffold comment 'reference value, not the proof' -> 'a brute-force check value, not the argument itself' to avoid posterior 'reference' vocabulary.
